2606.30105.title: Propagation of~Interval Belief Structures and~Imprecise Copulas for~Neural Network Verification
2606.30105.paper_summary: Quantitative verification of neural networks requires reasoning about probabilities under substantial uncertainty in both input distributions and their dependence structure. In realistic settings, this information is often only partially specified, and assuming precise probabilistic models can lead to unreliable results. We propose a sound framework for quantitative verification under imprecise probabilistic information, combining interval belief structures to represent marginal uncertainty with imprecise copulas to model uncertain dependence. We develop a propagation method for imprecisely coupled interval belief structures through feed-forward neural networks. Using mixed imprecise copula volumes, we derive sound push-forward constructions through affine transformations and activation functions. The resulting output can provide guaranteed lower and upper bounds on probabilistic safety properties, valid for all probability models compatible with the specified imprecise inputs.
2606.30105.paper_summary_zh: 量化驗證神經網絡需要在輸入分佈和其依賴結構的重大不確定性下推理概率。在現實情況中，這些信息通常僅部分被指定，假設精確的概率模型可能導致不可靠的結果。我們提出了一個在不精確概率信息下進行量化驗證的健全框架，結合了區間信念結構來表示邊際不確定性，並使用不精確的聯合分布來建模不確定的依賴。我們開發了一種通過前饋神經網絡對不精確耦合的區間信念結構進行傳播的方法。利用混合不精確聯合體積，我們通過仿射變換和激活函數推導出健全的推進構造。產生的輸出可以提供對概率安全性質的保證下限和上限，這對所有與指定的不精確輸入兼容的概率模型都是有效的。
2606.30105.author: Francesc Pifarre-Esquerda et.al.
2606.30105.authors: Francesc Pifarre-Esquerda, Eric Goubault, Sylvie Putot
